skill.name: api-route-design
skill.description: Design RESTful FastAPI routes with clear endpoint names, request validation, response models, pagination, filtering, sorting, and HTTP status codes.
skill.summary: Guides REST API route design for FastAPI and Python projects.
skill.author: Awais68

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many weak-cryptography and external-command alerts, but AI review found these to be false positives from REST examples, markdown code formatting, and regex validation in a local verifier. No prompt injection, network access, credential handling, command execution, or data exfiltration was found. The remaining risk is limited to a community-provided Python verifier script that reads local skill files.

low_findings.title: Static Weak-Cryptography Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged lines do not use cryptographic algorithms. They contain REST API terms, HTTP status guidance, regex parsing, or route examples used for documentation and validation.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Manual review found no crypto imports, hash functions, cipher calls, or password handling. The matches are documentation words and regex-based metadata checks.

low_findings.title: Static External-Command Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The flagged backtick patterns in SKILL.md are markdown inline code and fenced Python examples. They are not Ruby backtick execution and do not run shell commands.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The relevant file is markdown documentation, and the reviewed lines are code formatting or FastAPI examples. No shell interpreter, subprocess call, or command string is present there.

low_findings.title: Local Verification Script Reads Skill Files
low_findings.description: The package includes a Python verifier that accepts a skill path, reads SKILL.md, and checks for scripts/verify.py. This is legitimate validation logic, but executable community scripts should remain reviewable before publication.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The script clearly performs local filesystem checks and exits with validation status. It does not show network access, shell execution, environment secret access, or malicious persistence.

content.value_statement: REST API route design can become inconsistent when teams choose endpoints, schemas, and status codes ad hoc. This skill gives Claude, Codex, and Claude Code structured FastAPI guidance for predictable REST endpoints.
actual_capabilities: Recommends RESTful URL naming patterns for collections, single resources, and nested resources.
Shows FastAPI route patterns for GET, POST, PUT, PATCH, and DELETE endpoints.
Guides request validation with path parameters, query parameters, and Pydantic models.
Explains response model structure, error response formats, and common HTTP status codes.
Provides examples for pagination, filtering, sorting, and protected route dependencies.
limitations: Focuses on REST APIs and does not cover GraphQL or WebSocket design.
Examples are FastAPI-oriented and may need adaptation for other frameworks.
Does not implement database access layers or authentication systems by itself.
Does not replace project-specific security, performance, or compliance review.

use_cases.title: Create Consistent FastAPI Endpoints
use_cases.description: Design endpoint names, methods, request models, response models, and status codes for a new service resource.

use_cases.title: Review API Route Standards
use_cases.description: Check existing route plans against REST naming, pagination, filtering, sorting, and error response conventions.

use_cases.title: Prepare API Contracts for Frontend Work
use_cases.description: Define predictable response shapes and query parameters before client integration begins.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Basic Resource Route
prompt_templates.prompt: Design RESTful FastAPI routes for a {resource} resource. Include endpoint paths, HTTP methods, response models, and status codes.

prompt_templates.title: Add Validation and Errors
prompt_templates.prompt: Create FastAPI route guidance for {resource}. Include Pydantic create and update models, path validation, query validation, and consistent error responses.

prompt_templates.title: Plan List Operations
prompt_templates.prompt: Design list endpoints for {resource} with pagination, filtering, and sorting. Explain allowed query parameters, defaults, limits, and response fields.

prompt_templates.title: Review an API Design
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this FastAPI REST route design for {service}. Identify naming issues, status code problems, validation gaps, authorization assumptions, and OpenAPI documentation improvements.

output_examples.input: Design routes for a student fee resource.
output_examples.output: A route plan with collection and item endpoints, create and update operations, validation notes, response model guidance, and status code recommendations.

output_examples.input: Improve pagination and filtering for an item list endpoint.
output: Recommended query parameters with safe defaults.
A response shape that includes data, total count, limit, offset, and a has more indicator.
Notes on allowed sorting fields and validation constraints.

output_examples.input: Review an existing FastAPI route design.
output_examples.output: A concise review covering REST naming, method semantics, validation coverage, error responses, OpenAPI metadata, and authorization assumptions.

best_practices: Start with resource names, allowed operations, and expected response models before writing route handlers.
Use explicit validation for path, query, and body inputs, including limits for pagination and sorting.
Document success and error responses so OpenAPI output is useful for client developers.
anti_patterns: Do not mix singular and plural resource names for the same route family.
Do not accept unrestricted sorting fields or filter values from clients.
Do not return ad hoc error shapes that differ between endpoints.

faq.question: Does this skill generate complete FastAPI applications?
faq.answer: No. It focuses on route design patterns and endpoint structure, not full application scaffolding.

faq.question: Can it help with Pydantic models?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides guidance for create, update, response, pagination, and error models.

faq.question: Does it support GraphQL?
faq.answer: No. The skill is scoped to RESTful APIs and FastAPI-style route handlers.

faq.question: Can it choose HTTP status codes?
faq.answer: Yes. It explains common status codes for successful requests, validation errors, authorization failures, and missing resources.

faq.question: Does it include authentication implementation?
faq.answer: No. It can show dependency placement for protected routes, but it does not implement authentication.

faq.question: Is it useful for API reviews?
faq.answer: Yes. It can help review naming, method semantics, validation, response consistency, and OpenAPI documentation.
